title: Measure before optimization deterministic evidence bank

publication.changeSummary: Original misconception and checkpoint items retained; executable evidence is intentionally absent because Optimization decisions require profiler traces and a human justification, not a fixed pass/fail DOM state.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent standards, browser, and assessment review.

privateAuthorNotes: Human review must verify platform-version accuracy, equivalence, accessibility, and deterministic grading before formal use.
items.title: Measure before optimization: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this React: Component-Based Browser Applications misconception? Every rerender is a performance bug that should be prevented.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Rerenders are normal; optimize measured expensive work and verify the user-visible improvement.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Rerenders are normal; optimize measured expensive work and verify the user-visible improvement.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked React: Component-Based Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

options.text: Rerenders are normal; optimize measured expensive work and verify the user-visible improvement.
